query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan's ADIZ in May–June 2026:

**The Drop/Pause:** PLA air activity has been on a sustained decline through H1 2026, reverting to the "pre-Lai baseline." The most dramatic pause was **Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026** — ~9 days of *zero* PLA aircraft near the ADIZ (attributed to China's Two Sessions and Spring Festival).

**Typical daily counts (recent, May–June 2026):**
- June 2026 averaged **~7 sorties/day** (214 total for the month)
- Individual recent days: 2 (Jun 25-26), 6 (Jun 13), 14 (Jun 24-25), 28 (Jul 8-9)
- For context, 2025 averaged ~15/day

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** **~36 aircraft on March 17, 2026** — the peak within calendar-year 2026. No day in May or June came close (May stayed under 30 every day).

**Key caveat:** The record "90 median-line crossings / 130 aircraft" figure often cited belongs to the **late-December 2025 "Justice Mission-2025" exercise**, not 2026.

**Important nuance:** While air activity dropped sharply (June's 134 median-line crossings were well below 2024/2025 levels, with 12 days of no incursions), **naval activity hit a record June high of 225 PLAN vessels**. Analysts (K. Tristan Tang, AEI/ISW) stress this reflects a shift in the *mix* of coercion toward dispersed operations and naval pressure — not reduced capability or genuine de-escalation.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang — Monthly Update on PLA Air and Maritime Maneuvers Around Taiwan (June 2026)
sources.snippet: In June 2026, the PLA conducted 134 median line crossings and 214 total aircraft sorties detected around Taiwan... 225 PLAN vessels were detected during the month, marking the highest June total on record.

sources.title: K. Tristan Tang — Monthly Update on PLA Maneuvers Around Taiwan (May 2026)
sources.snippet: January through May 2026 was the lowest for the corresponding month since January 2023. No single day in May recorded more than 30 detected aircraft sorties. The last time daily sorties exceeded 30 was on March 17.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity
sources.snippet: From February 27th to March 5th, Taiwan's MND reported no PLA aircraft operating near Taiwan's ADIZ — an unusually long, extremely rare pause.

sources.title: China & Taiwan Update, July 2, 2026 (ISW–AEI)
sources.snippet: The PLA conducted 134 aerial incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ in June 2026... 12 days in June without any ADIZ incursions across the median line... reverted to the pre-Lai baseline since the start of 2026.

sources.title: Taiwan Maps and Dataviz — March 2026 PLA sortie summary
sources.snippet: In March 2026, 171 PLA aircraft sorties were recorded — lowest monthly figure since August 2022... peak of 36 aircraft recorded on March 17, 2026.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (Jun 26, 2026) — ROC MND
sources.snippet: 2 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN ships operating around Taiwan detected as of 6 a.m. today.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (Jun 25, 2026) — ROC MND
sources.snippet: 14 sorties of PLA aircraft, 6 PLAN ships and 2 official ships detected; 10 of 14 crossed the median line.

sources.title: PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan (Jul 9, 2026) — ROC MND
sources.snippet: 28 sorties of PLA aircraft, 8 PLAN ships and 5 official ships detected; 20 of 28 crossed the median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 6 PLA aircraft sorties, 8 PLAN vessels around territory (June 13, 2026)
sources.snippet: 6 sorties of PLA aircraft, 8 PLAN vessels and 2 official ships; 4 of 6 entered southwestern ADIZ.

sources.title: Taiwan Security Monitor — 90 sorties crossed median line (2nd highest on record, Dec 2025 drill)
sources.snippet: 90 sorties of PLA aircraft crossed the median line — second highest one-day total on record; 130 PLA aircraft detected (Justice Mission-2025 exercise, late Dec 2025).

sources.title: China flexes its military muscles around Taiwan (2025 annual totals)
sources.snippet: In 2025 the PLA conducted 5,317 sorties — a daily average of 15 — around Taiwan; median line crossed 3,867 times.

sources.title: PLA sends 8 vessels, 24 aircraft across median line (Mar 18, 2026 report on Mar 17 activity)
sources.snippet: 24 military aircraft detected crossing the median line between 6am March 17 and 6am March 18.

sources.title: Tracking China's Increased Military Activities in the Indo-Pacific in 2025 (CSIS ChinaPower)
sources.snippet: In 2025, China's military activity increased in the Taiwan Strait (published Feb 5, 2026).

key_facts: Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026: Taiwan MND reported ZERO PLA aircraft near the ADIZ for ~9 consecutive days — an unusually long pause attributed to China's Two Sessions and Spring Festival, not strategic shift.
May 2026: PLA aircraft sorties were the lowest for that calendar month since January 2023; no single day in May exceeded 30 detected sorties.
The last day before June with >30 sorties was March 17, 2026.
June 2026: 214 total aircraft sorties and 134 median-line crossings/ADIZ incursions detected — substantially lower than June 2024 and June 2025.
12 days in June 2026 had no ADIZ incursions across the median line (AEI/ISW).
June 2026 averaged roughly 7 aircraft sorties per day (214 ÷ 30).
Highest single-day PLA aircraft count in calendar-year 2026 was ~36 aircraft on March 17, 2026 (24 crossed the median line).
Naval activity rose even as air activity fell: 225 PLAN vessels detected in June 2026, the highest June total on record.
The record 90 median-line crossings / 130 aircraft single-day figure belongs to the late-December 2025 'Justice Mission-2025' exercise, NOT 2026.
For context, 2025 saw 5,317 total PLA sorties (~15/day average) and 3,867 median-line crossings.
March 2026 total of 171 monthly sorties was the lowest monthly figure since the post-Pelosi surge of August 2022.
Analysts (K. Tristan Tang, AEI/ISW) say the air drop does not indicate reduced capability; Beijing achieves similar effects via dispersed routine operations and rising naval activity.
